title: About Agra Orthopaedic Society
summary: A professional home for orthopaedic learning, ethical practice, fellowship, and public service in Agra since 1979.

sections.title: A society built around shared learning
paragraphs: Agra Orthopaedic Society brings orthopaedic surgeons and trainees together through academic meetings, continuing medical education, skills development, research, professional fellowship, and community awareness.
The society connects local clinical experience with wider academic collaboration while preserving the professional legacy built by generations of orthopaedic surgeons in Agra.

sections.title: What guides the society
bullets: Advance orthopaedic knowledge through regular academic activity.
Encourage ethical, evidence-informed, and compassionate patient care.
Support postgraduate education, presentation, research, and mentorship.
Contribute to public understanding of bone, joint, injury, and mobility health.
Strengthen fellowship and collaboration across the orthopaedic community.

title: Membership
summary: Join a professional community centred on learning, fellowship, ethics, and service.

sections.title: For orthopaedic professionals in and around Agra
paragraphs: AOS membership is intended for orthopaedic surgeons, postgraduate trainees, and professionals connected with the society’s academic and ethical objectives.
For current eligibility, fee details, and document requirements, contact the society office. Applications remain subject to verification and approval under AOS rules.

sections.title: What membership supports
bullets: Academic meetings, CME programmes, and workshops.
Professional fellowship across institutions and generations.

sections.title: AOS Digital Directory 2026–2027
paragraphs: AOS is updating its official member records through a consent-led digital directory. Directory submissions support member communication, academic activity, society records, and an accurate professional network for the current term.

The directory is intended for circulation among AOS members. It will not be published as an open website directory until secure member access and the society’s final verification process are in place.

sections.title: Members stay in control
bullets: Each member chooses which professional details may appear in the directory.
Photograph permission is recorded separately from general directory consent.
Family details and family photographs remain limited to internal family or social communication where permission was given.
Members may contact the society office to correct or update their official record.

title: Public Awareness
summary: Clear, responsible starting points for bone, joint, injury, and mobility health.

sections.title: Health information, not a diagnosis
paragraphs: Good bone and joint health begins with regular movement, appropriate strength work, balanced nutrition, injury prevention, and timely assessment when symptoms persist.
General information cannot replace examination or individual medical advice. For urgent injury, severe pain, weakness, numbness, deformity, or loss of function, seek qualified medical care promptly.

sections.title: Daily Hindi public-awareness posts
paragraphs: AOS will use its Facebook page for regular Hindi public-awareness posts on common orthopaedic problems. The aim is to share one clear, practical topic at a time for patients and families in Agra.

Suggested themes include osteoporosis, knee arthritis, back pain, fracture first aid, sports injury prevention, senior-citizen falls, road-safety trauma prevention, posture, safe exercise, and when to seek urgent orthopaedic care.

title: President Message
summary: A current note from the President of Agra Orthopaedic Society.
